title: InterBank Networking & LGBT+ History Month event - 19 February 2026
location: event and used for future InterBank communications
description: InterBank invites you to join us for an exciting evening of networking to celebrate LGBT+ History Month.  Mark your calendars for Thursday, 19 February 2026, from 6:00 PM at Bar Soho 23-25 Old Compton Street, London, W1D 5JL . This month's theme is Love is Love in celebration of LGBT+ history.  We're certainly looking forward to seeing you and please say hi to any of our representatives wearing InterBank badges upon arrival if you're new to our events.  InterBank is dedicated to creating an inclusive and safe space for the LGBT+ community. We expect all guests to adhere to our code of conduct. While this venue is not wheelchair accessible, most of our monthly networking events are. Please register here. Friends, partners, and allies are welcome – one ticket per person. Photos may be taken at the event and used for future InterBank communications. We look forward to seeing you there.

title: InterBank Networking & Neurodiversity Celebration Week - 19 March 2026
location: Law Society, 113 Chancery Lane, London, WC2A 1PL
description: InterBank invites you to join us for an exciting evening of networking to celebrate Neurodiversity Celebration Week.  Mark your calendars for Thursday, 19 March 2026, from 6:00 PM at the Law Society, 113 Chancery Lane, London, WC2A 1PL. This event is focused on Neurodiversity Celebration Week (16-20 March). It is a worldwide initiative that challenges stereotypes and misconceptions about neurological differences. As part of highlighting this celebration week, we will have a quiet space set aside and open to all.  Additionally, our Women of InterBank group is organising women and non-binary people dedicated drinks at The Last Judgment (right around the corner) between 18:30 and 19:30, so feel free to join us if you’re interested. We will also be joined by Emily Jackson, Senior Disability Business Partner from the Business Disability Forum. Emily works closely with BDF Partner organisations to enable them to achieve their strategic aims on disability inclusion and to adopt leading practice across all areas of the business. BDF works with business and government to remove barriers and advance disability inclusion, promoting equitable access to employment, products, and services for disabled people. We're certainly looking forward to seeing you and please say hi to any of our representatives wearing InterBank badges upon arrival if you're new to our events. In addition, feel free to connect with one of our dedicated hosts from the InterBank committee from the Instagram post of the event.  InterBank is dedicated to creating an inclusive and safe space for the LGBT+ community. We expect all guests to adhere to our code of conduct. Note that this venue is also wheelchair accessible. Please register here. Friends, partners, and allies are welcome – one ticket per person. Photos may be taken at the event and used for future InterBank communications. We look forward to seeing you there.

title: InterBank Networking - 18 June
description: InterBank invites you to join us for an exciting evening of networking and Pride month celebration.  Mark your calendars for Thursday, 18 June 2026, from 6:00 PM at the Pacific Ballroom at the Pan Pacific Hotel, 80 Houndsditch, London EC3A 7AB. **Note: the ballroom entrance is a separate glass enclosed entrance on the right side of the plaza when facing the hotel.  We will also be joined by Richard Angell, CEO of Terrance Higgins Trust, the UK's leading HIV charity which has been shaping public health responses and supporting those living with HIV since the early 1980s. We'll hear from Richard about the great work they are doing on improving sexual health and advocating for HIV support and campaigning for an end to new cases. This Pride month, help us make a difference by contributing to their outstanding work through the Do It For Terry campaign. THT representatives will be on hand throughout the evening to accept donations to this important cause.  We're certainly looking forward to seeing you and please say hi to any of our representatives wearing InterBank badges upon arrival if you're new to our events. In addition, feel free to connect with one of our dedicated hosts from the InterBank committee from the Instagram post of the event.  InterBank is dedicated to creating an inclusive and safe space for the LGBT+ community. We expect all guests to adhere to our code of conduct. Note that this venue is wheelchair accessible and offers a separate quiet room for those needing a break. Please register here. Friends, partners, and allies are welcome – one ticket per person. Photos may be taken at the event and used for future InterBank communications. We look forward to seeing you there.
